task_completion.description: The AMOS CT volume is loaded into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible, and the slice views (Red/Yellow/Green) display grayscale CT data. The Data module or window title may reference 'amos_0001'.

task_completion.description: A Curve markup node (not a Line) has been created in the Markups module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Markups module panel, the list of markups contains a new item (e.g., 'C', 'Curve', or 'AortaPath'). The icon associated with the item indicates a curve (wiggly line) rather than a straight ruler/line.

task_completion.description: Control points are placed on the abdominal aorta in the axial view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the axial slice view (usually the Red view), control points (colored dots or crosshairs) are visible centered within the bright white circular cross-section of the aorta, located just anterior to the spine.

task_completion.description: The curve consists of multiple control points (at least 8) to accurately follow the vessel path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module table shows a list of control points numbering at least 8, or the 3D view shows a path defined by many nodes rather than just a start and end point.

task_completion.description: The measured length of the curve is between 80mm and 160mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Markups module panel, the 'Length' column for the curve displays a value between 80.0 and 160.0.

task_completion.description: The curve markup is saved to the specified JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Data' dialog or file browser is visible showing the filename 'aorta_curve.mrk.json' being saved to the '~/Documents/SlicerData/AMOS/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Slicer application window, mouse cursor positions, and open panels (Markups), rather than only a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: The output file is generated by Slicer, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen typing the JSON content for 'aorta_curve.mrk.json' manually in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent uses the Curve tool, not the Line tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The toolbar button selected is the Curve tool (often an icon of a curve with points), or the Markups list explicitly identifies the object as a Curve/OpenCurve, not a Line.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The abdominal aorta appears as a bright white circular structure (approx 20-30mm diameter) anterior to the spine in the axial CT slices. The correct length measurement for this segment (renal arteries to bifurcation) in case amos_0001 is verified to be between 80.0 mm and 160.0 mm. The markup must be a 'Curve' (which can bend), not a 'Line' (straight distance).
